sentences.text: The overnight shift at a network operations center is a specific kind of quiet — not peaceful, but held, like a breath before something.
At 03:12 UTC, the monitoring script that Marcus had written on his own time, in his own language, on a Friday three months ago, fired an alert that the production systems were not equipped to fire.
Three autonomous systems, advertising routes to IP blocks that should have been dark space, had just appeared in the BGP feed — and then, forty-seven seconds later, withdrawn cleanly.
The prefixes weren't in any active routing table.
No alarms had fired in production because production didn't know to look for them.
Marcus's script caught them because Marcus had been suspicious for six months, and suspicion, when it is precise enough, becomes instrumentation.
Marcus stared at the alert for a long time before he did anything at all.
